document_id: Ombudsman Listed by Area Agency on Aging

findings.description: Table header row styling differs - PDF uses gold/yellow background for header row, HTML uses plain styling
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Region | Area Agency on Aging | Regional Ombudsman | Counties Served' with gold background; HTML shows same headers without background color

findings.description: Office header formatting differs slightly - PDF centers the header block, HTML left-aligns it with a heading style and red underline
findings.evidence: PDF: centered block with bold 'Office of the State Long Term Care Ombudsman'; HTML: left-aligned large heading with horizontal rule underneath

findings.description: PDF page 1 has centered layout for office info block, HTML page 2 uses left-aligned layout
findings.evidence: PDF centers address, phone, fax, website; HTML left-aligns them

findings.description: Table cell content alignment differs - PDF centers text in cells, HTML left-aligns
findings.evidence: Region letters, agency names, ombudsman names, and counties are centered in PDF but left-aligned in HTML

findings.description: The 'Previously Isothermal Commission' note is in italics in the PDF but uses parenthetical plain text format in HTML for Region C
findings.evidence: PDF: italic '(Previously Isothermal Commission)'; HTML: '(Previously Isothermal Commission)' appears in regular text with parentheses

findings.description: Page break differences cause Region E to appear at top of PDF page 3 but mid-page in HTML page 4
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 starts with Region E; HTML continues from Region D into E on the same scrollable page

findings.description: Hyperlinks present in the PDF are not rendered as clickable <a> tags in the HTML. The website URL and all email addresses are presented as plain text rather than as hyperlinks.
findings.evidence: PDF contains clickable links like 'https://www.ncdhhs.gov/aging', 'renee.kea@dhhs.nc.gov', 'anna@regiona.org', etc. HTML renders these as plain text without <a> tags.

findings.description: The document uses only one heading (h1) for the entire document. The document title 'Division of Aging / North Carolina Long Term Care Ombudsman Program' from the PDF is not used as the h1; instead 'Office of the State Long Term Care Ombudsman' is the h1. The program title appears only in a page-header div. There are no h2 or lower headings to structure the regional sections.
findings.evidence: PDF title is 'Division of Aging / North Carolina Long Term Care Ombudsman Program' appearing at top of every page. HTML h1 is 'Office of the State Long Term Care Ombudsman' which is a sub-section in the PDF.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements - a structural duplication. The outer <main> from the template wraps an inner <main> element containing the actual content.
findings.evidence: HTML has <body><main>...<main>...</main>...</main></body> - two nested main elements.

findings.description: The page header 'Division of Aging / North Carolina Long Term Care Ombudsman Program' is repeated both in the page-header div and implicitly as context, and the footer 'As of February 2026' appears both within the content section and in the page-footer div. This mirrors the PDF's repeating headers/footers but results in duplication in a single-page HTML context.
findings.evidence: HTML has 'As of February 2026' in the section content AND in the page-footer div. PDF repeats this on every page as a footer.

findings.description: Minor formatting artifact in Region P's phone/fax information where the fax number is oddly split across lines in the HTML, though this reflects the PDF source text.
findings.evidence:
HTML: '(252) 638-3185 ext.
3010 F: (252)
638-3187' - fax number split. PDF also shows similar formatting: '(252) 638-3185 ext. 3010 F: (252) 638-3187'
